commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

commit.message:
fix: URL-encode ESXi password in OVF deployment vi:// URL (merges PR #313, addresses #312)

fix: URL-encode ESXi password and use self references in OVF deployment

The ovf_deployment template had two issues:
1. Passwords with URL-special characters (e.g., #, @, ?) broke the
   vi:// URL passed to ovftool. Now URL-encoded via python3 urllib.
2. Destroy provisioner referenced var.* which Terraform forbids in
   destroy-time provisioners. Now stores connection details in
   triggers_replace and uses self.triggers_replace.* throughout.

commit.message:
Fix touch drag-and-drop: use refs to avoid stale closure bug

The previous implementation read touchOverDay from React state inside
onTouchEnd, which was stale because native DOM listeners and React state
updates don't synchronise instantly. Fixed by:
- touchOverDayRef (ref) stores the current target day, always up-to-date
- touchMovedRef (ref) tracks whether the finger actually moved (drag vs tap)
- e.preventDefault() in onTouchEnd during a drag suppresses the phantom
  click event that would otherwise open the project editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: detect upstream skill updates in sf-skills-status (#358)

* fix: detect upstream skill updates in sf-skills-status

The status command was comparing installed SHA against the manifest SHA
(recorded at last sync), meaning "up to date" only meant "not locally
modified since last refresh" — it was blind to newer upstream versions.

On macOS the menubar compensated by doing a background git fetch, but on
Linux there is no such process, so the status could silently show
"up to date" indefinitely.

Fix: before printing the table, fetch the upstream skills cache and
compare each managed skill's installed file against the corresponding
file in the cache. If they differ, report "update available" instead
of "up to date". Falls back gracefully when the cache does not exist
yet and warns the user to run sf-skills-refresh.

* fix: use git show to read upstream ref instead of working-tree files

After git fetch origin, the working tree still contains the old
checked-out files. Only remote-tracking refs are updated. The previous
code compared the installed SHA against the working-tree copy, which
meant update available was never reported even when upstream had changed.

Switch to git show <remote-ref>:<path> so we read the file content
from the fetched remote branch directly. Also detect the remote default
branch dynamically via symbolic-ref and ensure the SHA hash tool
matches what compute_sha256 uses (shasum on macOS, sha256sum on Linux).

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify upstream detection with git pull --ff-only

Replace git fetch + git show origin/<ref>:path with git pull --ff-only
so the working tree is updated directly. This eliminates:
- CACHE_REF detection via git symbolic-ref
- Duplicated shasum/sha256sum logic (reuses compute_sha256)
- ~15 lines of branching logic

The cache dir is fully managed by sparkdock, so ff-only pull is safe.

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix barcode detection: remove PURE_BARCODE hint causing all decodes to fail

ZXing checks hints.containsKey(PURE_BARCODE), not the value. Setting
PURE_BARCODE to Boolean.FALSE still enabled pure barcode mode, which
requires the barcode to fill most of the image and fails on label
images with surrounding text. Removing the key entirely fixes QR code
detection on all tested label images.

Confirmed: PURE_BARCODE key present -> NOT FOUND on both region and
full image. Key absent -> FOUND immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove all hardcoded .pathfinder/ project-level paths (#26)

## Summary

Several scripts and hooks hardcoded `.pathfinder/` at the project root,
but `pathfinder-init.py` creates data at `<testDir>/pathfinder/` (e.g.,
`tests/pathfinder/`). This mismatch caused silent failures when the test
directory wasn't the project root.

## Changes

| File | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| `generate-ui-test.py` | Hardcoded `.pathfinder/config.json` | Walks
project tree to find `pathfinder/config.json` |
| `.githooks/pre-commit` | Only checked `.pathfinder/journeys.json` |
Finds all `**/pathfinder/journeys.json` files |
| `generate-diagrams.py` | Usage docstring said `.pathfinder/` | Updated
to `<testDir>/pathfinder/` |
| `ui-testing.md` | `.pathfinder/baselines/` and
`.pathfinder/config.json` | `<testDir>/pathfinder/` |
| `package.json` | `.pathfinder/journeys.json` |
`pathfinder-workspace/pathfinder/journeys.json` with `--output-dir` |

## Remaining `.pathfinder` references

Only migration-related (install script + docs mentioning legacy path
migration). Zero functional references.

## Test plan

- [ ] `generate-ui-test.py` finds config in
`tests/pathfinder/config.json` (not just `.pathfinder/`)
- [ ] Pre-commit hook validates `tests/pathfinder/journeys.json`
- [ ] `npm run init` creates data in `pathfinder-workspace/pathfinder/`
- [ ] `npm run coverage` and `npm run diagrams` use correct paths

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: normalize file paths in fingerprint store to prevent duplicate rows

Paths arriving from initial_scan (via glob) and watchdog events can
differ in symlink resolution, tilde expansion, and '..' components,
causing the same physical file to be stored under multiple keys.
Resolve all paths to their canonical form at the fingerprint store
boundary so callers never need to think about normalization.

Also enables WAL journal mode and NORMAL synchronous writes for better
concurrent read performance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add CEFR level filtering for focused vocabulary practice (#49)

* Add CEFR level filter to home screen for focused practice

Places prominent A1/A2/B1/B2 chips directly on the home screen above
the Start Practice button, where they're immediately visible and tappable.
Chips are multi-select and persist across sessions via settings. The
selected levels control both which new words are introduced and which
due cards appear in the daily session, consistent with the existing
category filter behaviour. When only one level is selected, deselecting
it is blocked so the session always has words.

The level selector only renders when multiple levels exist in the DB,
so current users (A1-only) see no change until A2 words are seeded.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017K1xdX4sY96qD3k91CyvfQ

* fix: translation quotes

* Fix level filter to use cumulative CEFR semantics; protect seed script from level regression

Selecting A2 in the level filter now includes A1 words (expandLevelsCumulative),
matching how Goethe wordbooks stack — each level includes all prior levels.
Selecting A1+B1 also fills in A2 automatically.

csv-to-pocketbase.js: when a word already exists at a lower level (e.g. A1),
running the script for A2 data no longer overwrites it. The existing level is
preserved so 'first introduced at' semantics stay intact across seed runs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017K1xdX4sY96qD3k91CyvfQ

* Merge sources array when updating existing nouns in seed script

Previously the update would overwrite the existing sources with only
the current run's source list. Now sources are unioned so a word that
appears in both Goethe A1 and A2 lists accumulates both entries.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017K1xdX4sY96qD3k91CyvfQ

* chore: add .http extension to gitignore

* chore: remove console log

* refactor: update level selection logic and adjust CSV import for A2

Modified the level selection in HomeScreen to allow cumulative selection of levels up to the tapped one, enhancing user experience. Updated the CSV import script to read from 'goethe-a2.csv' instead of 'goethe-a1.csv', ensuring the correct data is processed for the A2 level.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(wq-915): fix arithmetic expression error in 23-outcome-feedback.sh

grep -c returns 0 with exit code 1 when no matches found. Under
set -euo pipefail, `grep -c ... || echo 0` produces "0\n0" (grep's
output concatenated with echo's fallback), causing bash arithmetic
syntax errors. Replace `|| echo 0` with `|| true` which suppresses
the non-zero exit without duplicating the output.

commit.message:
docs: clarify that result.output requires a schema key named "output" (#92)

When using createSmithers() with schema keys other than "output" (e.g.
"page", "analysis"), result.output is always undefined. This is easy to
miss and leads to confusion — task outputs are persisted to SQLite but
not returned on the RunResult.

Added a dedicated section explaining:
- When result.output is vs isn't populated
- How to query SQLite directly for non-"output" schema keys
- The alternative of naming your schema key "output"

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

gives different discount percentages), compute a single target discount
from the new total vs total list price and apply it to every line equally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: prevent duplicate prescriptions across active sales

Compute a SHA-256 hash of the prescription file on drop and store it on
the order document. Before creating a new order, query Firestore for any
active (non-cancelled, non-soft-deleted) order with the same hash. If a
duplicate is found, block order creation and show an error with a link
to the existing order.

supervisor: status #54 + hourly #9, stalled ~8hrs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: HivePartitionCheck — Ansible copy module per SQL file (evita ARG_MAX)

[Errno 7] Argument list too long: '/bin/sh' — Ansible passava il contenuto
di shell: | come argomento a sh -c. Con migliaia di printf (una per tabella)
superava ARG_MAX (tipicamente 131072 byte).

Fix: il contenuto SQL viene trasferito via modulo Ansible copy (nessun limite
di dimensione), e shell: contiene solo il breve comando beeline -f.

Nuova architettura:
- _build_beeline_conn(hive_cfg, user): stringa connessione beeline riutilizzabile
- _build_show_tables_sql(databases): puro SQL (no shell), per Ansible copy
- _build_show_partitions_sql(db_tables): puro SQL (no shell), per Ansible copy
- _run_playbook_with_sql(sql_content, conn_str): playbook con copy+shell+file tasks

Rimossi _build_show_tables_script e _build_show_partitions_script (printf-based,
soggetti ad ARG_MAX). Il debug del SQL file avviene ora localmente in Python
(niente cat >&2 nel playbook).

102 test totali (tutti verdi).

Fix 403 error handling: show actionable message instead of force re-login

403 on /playlists/{id}/tracks is a permissions issue (Spotify Development
Mode), not a token problem. Clearing the token and redirecting to login
created an infinite loop. Now shows a toast directing the user to add
their Spotify email under developer.spotify.com → app Settings → User
Management. Session is preserved so the user doesn't need to re-login.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Filter DA terminal responses to prevent visible garbage text

xterm.js responds to tmux's Device Attributes queries (ESC[c), but
the responses arrive as session_input and leak as visible text like
"1;2c0;276;0c". Strip these CSI DA responses before writing to PTY.
